symptoms.symptom_name: Movement-triggered vertigo beginning during turbulent boat motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vertigo whose onset coincides exactly with passive locomotion and which is provoked by head movement is the defining feature of motion sickness.
A clear kinetic trigger favours a benign cause but does not by itself exclude a coincident central event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable nausea and vomiting with only fleeting relief after each episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hyperactive vagal emetic response is characteristic of severe motion sickness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Autonomic prodrome — yawning, pallor, cold sweating and hypersalivation preceding the vertigo

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The stereotyped autonomic prodrome (yawning, cold sweat, hypersalivation, pallor) is highly characteristic of motion sickness.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological symptoms — no sudden severe headache, unilateral weakness/numbness, dysarthria or diplopia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficits lowers the probability of a central cause but does not exclude an isolated cerebellar infarct, which can present with vertigo alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Total inability to tolerate oral fluids for several hours, living alone (dehydration risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks severity and drives disposition (parenteral antiemetic, hydration, in-person review) rather than pointing to a specific diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focused neurological examination normal — no spontaneous nystagmus at rest, no dysmetria on finger-to-nose, cranial nerves grossly intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focused neurological examination is the key discriminator excluding a central cause — a sealed clinician finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain, which is precisely why in-person assessment is required.
A normal neurological examination is consistent with a benign peripheral/vestibular cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild resting tachycardia (HR ~102) with otherwise stable vitals, consistent with early volume depletion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia reflects distress and early volume depletion — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers dehydration risk from the inability to retain fluids, not from a measured heart rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no diarrhoea and no abdominal cramping; vertigo preceded the nausea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of diarrhoea, fever and cramping, with vertigo preceding the vomiting, argues against a primary gastrointestinal cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear bilious vomitus with no blood or coffee-ground material despite forceful retching

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of haematemesis argues against a retching-induced upper-GI bleed (Mallory-Weiss) but does not itself confirm the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Otoscopy normal — clear external canals and intact, normal tympanic membranes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal tympanic membranes make a middle-ear/otitis-related labyrinthitis less likely — a sealed clinician finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Movement-triggered vertigo with a stereotyped autonomic prodrome and vomiting, and no focal neurological symptoms, together identify a benign vestibular cause — but confirmation still requires an in-person neurological examination to exclude a central mimic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person focused neurological examination (including nystagmus assessment, finger-to-nose, gait, and the HINTS battery where feasible)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The essential test to exclude a posterior-circulation (cerebellar) stroke; it cannot be performed via telehealth and is the reason urgent same-day in-person assessment is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent CT/MRI brain (posterior fossa) if any neurological red flag or a HINTS pattern suggesting a central lesion emerges
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Neuroimaging is indicated only if the in-person examination or red-flag screen suggests a central cause; not a routine step in benign kinetosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Point-of-care assessment of hydration and, if concern, electrolytes (in the in-person setting)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Prolonged vomiting with no oral intake can cause dehydration and electrolyte disturbance; assessed at the in-person review, not remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine early brain imaging (MRI) in a stable patient with a clear kinetic trigger and a normal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated when the trigger is clear, vitals are stable and the focused neurological examination is normal; ordering it reflexively would be over-investigation.
